ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general mushroom soup entry (FOODON_00002539); no specific entry for truffle-infused creamy mushroom soup with focaccia croutons. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant bowl of creamy mushroom soup, approximately 300 g, served with focaccia croutons and a drizzle of truffle oil.

nutrient_source.title: Cream of mushroom soup, canned, condensed — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; supplemented with USDA data for truffle oil and focaccia bread components
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact match for restaurant-prepared creamy forest mushroom soup with truffle oil and focaccia croutons in any approved primary source. Values estimated by component analysis: cream of mushroom soup base (USDA FDC #06900 and related entries) adjusted for added cream, truffle-infused oil, and focaccia crouton contribution. Energy, macros, and sodium estimated from weighted component proportions. Values represent a plausible per-100g profile for a restaurant-style cream mushroom soup with enrichments.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of cream soups and vegetable-based soups — analogous estimate based on cream of mushroom soup and similar cream-based vegetable soups
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for creamy forest mushroom soup with truffle oil. GI estimated at 40 based on analogous cream-based vegetable soups listed in the University of Sydney GI Database (GI range 30–50 for cream soups with low-to-moderate starch content). Focaccia croutons contribute modest starch but are a minor component by weight. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 15.0) / 100 = 6.0, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving ≈ 15.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~320 mg per 100 g; ~960 mg per 300 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its high sodium content (~960 mg per serving), which represents approximately 40–48% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000–2400 mg; request reduced-salt preparation or consume a smaller portion.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load from focaccia croutons and cream base; low GI (~40) mitigates acute glycaemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low glycaemic index (~40) and moderate glycaemic load (~6 per serving) make this soup relatively suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, but the cream and butter content adds saturated fat which may impair insulin sensitivity; portion control and omission of croutons are advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from cream and butter (~2.8 g per 100 g; ~8.4 g per serving) and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of elevated saturated fat (~8.4 g per serving) and high sodium (~960 mg per serving) poses a moderate cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size, request preparation with reduced cream and no added butter, and favour the truffle oil component over saturated fat sources.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Crema di Funghi Tartufata is an Italian-style creamy forest mushroom soup, served at Ricciotti restaurant in Singapore. The dish consists of a velvety, blended cream soup made from a medley of forest mushrooms (such as porcini, shiitake, and button mushrooms) sautéed with onion, garlic, and butter, then simmered in vegetable stock and enriched with heavy cream to achieve a smooth, rich texture. The soup is finished with a drizzle of aromatic truffle oil, which imparts a distinctive earthy, luxurious fragrance, and served with focaccia croutons that add a crisp, herbed textural contrast. The dish is vegetarian (marked V on the menu) and contains gluten and dairy allergens. Nutritionally, it is moderate in energy (~72 kcal per 100 g), relatively high in fat from cream and truffle oil, and notably high in sodium (~320 mg per 100 g), making sodium management important for hypertensive individuals. The low glycaemic index (~40) makes it a reasonable choice for blood glucose management, though the saturated fat content warrants attention for cardiovascular health. The dish is priced at SGD 17.44 at Ricciotti - The Riverwalk, Singapore, and represents a classic Italian trattoria-style starter course.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional match found in any approved primary source for this specific restaurant-prepared dish. Nutrient values estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for cream of mushroom soup, cream, truffle oil, and focaccia bread. GI estimated from analogous cream-based vegetable soups in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the component-estimation approach rather than direct measurement.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component analysis (cream of mushroom soup base, heavy cream, truffle oil, focaccia croutons) as no exact approved-source entry exists for this restaurant dish. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reliably reported in the component sources used for estimation. 3. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources used. 4. Trans fat is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in cream-based soups per USDA data. 5. GI of 40 is an analogous estimate based on cream-based vegetable soups from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific dish. 6. The food_group has been corrected from the input value 'Grains and staples' to 'Mixed', as this dish is a cream soup with multiple food group components (dairy, vegetables, bread). 7. Multilingual local_names for Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are not provided as this is an Italian dish served in Singapore; Chinese names are provided as a courtesy for the Singapore market context. 8. The (V) suffix in the original food name denotes vegetarian; is_vegetarian is set to true accordingly.
